description: The original pack. 10 questions. You probably know 3 of them.

questions.hostIntro: Buckle up, brainiac. It's science time — and no, we don't care that you hate science.
questions.question: Which planet in our solar system has the most moons?
questions.hostCommentary: SATURN. With 146 confirmed moons, it wins the moon contest. Jupiter is a close second and very jealous.

questions.hostIntro: Here's one for the people who claim they don't watch TV but somehow know everything about TV.
questions.question: What is the name of the coffee shop in the TV show Friends?
questions.hostCommentary: Central Perk! You all knew that. Don't pretend you didn't.

questions.hostIntro: Put down your phone. No, seriously. We can tell when you're cheating. Put it down.
questions.question: In what year did the Berlin Wall fall?
questions.hostCommentary: 1989! Also the year Taylor Swift was born. Coincidence? Probably, yes.

questions.hostIntro: Everyone's an expert on food. Let's find out how right you are.
questions.question: Which country invented the croissant?
questions.hostCommentary: AUSTRIA. The croissant was invented in Vienna. France just made it buttery and took all the credit. Classic France.

questions.hostIntro: This question is brought to you by every argument you've had about tech at the dinner table.
questions.question: What does 'HTTP' stand for?
questions.hostCommentary: HyperText Transfer Protocol! You use it literally every second of your life and now you finally know what it means.

questions.hostIntro: Nature! Terrible, beautiful, deeply weird nature.
questions.question: A group of flamingos is called a what?
questions.hostCommentary: A FLAMBOYANCE. Of course it is. These birds knew their brand before branding was a thing.

questions.hostIntro: Words are going to fly at you. When you see one that connects to the clue, BUZZ IN. Wrong answer? You lose points AND you sit out for three seconds. No pressure.
questions.question: THINGS THAT COME IN DOZENS
questions.hostCommentary: Eggs, donuts, roses, months, apostles, and jury members. Baguettes and beer cans were traps. You're welcome.

questions.hostIntro: Two buckets. One decision. Try not to embarrass yourself.
questions.question: Dog Breed or Cheese?
questions.hostCommentary: Brie, Gouda, Muenster, and Gruyere are cheeses. Labrador, Dachshund, Corgi, and Vizsla are dogs. Muenster had people fooled.

questions.hostIntro: 60 SECONDS. True or false. As many as you can. Up for true, down for false. GO GO GO.
questions.question: True or False? Answer as many as you can!
answers: The Great Wall of China is visible from space
A day on Venus is shorter than a year on Venus
Lightning never strikes the same place twice
Mt. Everest is the tallest mountain from Earth's core
Napoleon Bonaparte was unusually short for his time
questions.hostCommentary: How'd you do? Fun fact: Napoleon was actually average height. Propaganda is old.

questions.hostIntro: An image is being revealed. The sooner you buzz in, the more points you get. Wrong early? Big penalty. Your call.
questions.question: What famous structure is this?
questions.hostCommentary: The Colosseum! Built in 70 AD in Rome. Still holds up better than most things built last year.
